events.title: Global press freedom falls to lowest level in 25 years, RSF warns
events.description: Reporters Without Borders says global press freedom has dropped to its lowest level in 25 years, with only 1 percent of the world’s population living in countries rated as having a “good” media environment. The report highlights rising violence against journalists in conflict zones, worsening legal pressure worldwide, and political attacks on the media, while Norway ranks first and Eritrea remains last.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global press freedom falls to lowest level in 25 years, RSF warns

events.title: AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns
events.description: With spending on advertising topping $1 trillion a year worldwide, the United Nations on Wednesday highlighted the untapped power of major brands to shape the future of Artificial Intelligence, warning that a failure to act could deepen a global information integrity crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council
events.description: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the Security Council is debating maritime security with the UN chief calling on nations restricting ship movement through the Strait of Hormuz to “let the global economy breathe.” Follow in-depth meeting coverage here. Stay with us for live updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council

events.title: Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence'
events.description: Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement – but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

events.title: 'The world deserves food sovereignty and more secured food access for everybody'
events.description: As geopolitical instability exposes the deep entanglement between energy, food systems, and global security, Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Delphine Lévi Alvarès, Petrochemicals Campaign Manager at the Center for International Environmental Law (CIEL). Joining us from a high-level summit in Colombia, Ms. Alvarès says we've reached a critical juncture that requires steadfast action. The on-going conflict and instability in the Middle East, she argues, is less an “opportunity” than a moral and systemic reckoning: “a stark reminder that we have to decouple our economies from fossil fuels.”

events.title: Queensland rejects key Bondi report recommendation as Albanese’s gun buyback flounders
events.description: State’s police minister says buyback ‘doesn’t focus on keeping guns out of the hands of terrorists and criminals’, leaving NSW only clear supporter of plan Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Queensland has rejected key recommendations from the Bondi royal commission’s interim report, insisting plans for a national gun buyback will not keep weapons “out of the hands of terrorists and criminals”. The report, handed down by commissioner Virginia Bell on Thursday, raised doubts about whether efforts to establish a national gun register after the 2022 police killings at Wieambilla in Queensland had been “unduly leisurely”. Bell recommended the federal government and the states speed up a jointly funded weapons buyback scheme. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Queensland rejects key Bondi report recommendation as Albanese’s gun buyback flounders

events.title: New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah
events.description: The New IRA reportedly claimed a car bomb attack on a Belfast police station, threatening further strikes amid alleged Iran and Hezbollah connections.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah

events.title: The Bondi terror report raises more questions than answers about the massacre - and illuminated its horror
events.description: The 155-page interim report released on Thursday shows how little is known – and can be shared – about the 14 December shooting Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast If there’s one thing that’s clear from the royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion’s 155-page interim report , it’s how much about the Bondi massacre remains unknown – and how little of what is known can be shared with the public. More than a third of the recommendations from the report – which was released on Thursday – were confidential, although the Albanese government plans to implement all of them. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 The Bondi terror report raises more questions than answers about the massacre - and illuminated its horror

events.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
events.description: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the Maritime Security Centre Indian Ocean (MSCIO), the tracking service of the EU’s naval force. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week

events.title: Deadly migration route leaves Horn of Africa migrants stranded
events.description: Migrants in Djibouti are making the long journey back home after failed attempts to cross the Bab-el-Mandeb Strait to reach Gulf countries, facing hunger, detention and life-threatening conditions. Driven by poverty and conflict in Ethiopia, thousands attempt this perilous route each year, with more than 900 deaths recorded in 2025, making it the deadliest on record.

events.title: 2015 nuclear deal ‘no basis’ for any new agreement with Iran
events.description: The 2015 nuclear accord with Iran cannot be the starting point for a new agreement with the country, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said on Wednesday in New York.

events.title: Anti-gun violence nonprofit founder and ex-MMA fighter convicted in fatal 2024 shooting murder
events.description: Ex-MMA fighter who founded an anti-violence nonprofit focused on gun crime was convicted of murder in the shooting death of a man in Colorado.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Anti-gun violence nonprofit founder and ex-MMA fighter convicted in fatal 2024 shooting murder

events.title: World News in Brief: Syria human rights update, Cuba post-hurricane support still vital, impunity and violence in Myanmar
events.description: Three mass graves were recently uncovered in northeastern Syria, including one reportedly at the site of a former detention centre run by the Kurdish-backed Syrian Armed Forces (SDF).
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ World News in Brief: Syria human rights update, Cuba post-hurricane support still vital, impunity and violence in Myanmar

events.title: Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns
events.description: Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance – “an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community,” a senior official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF has said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns

events.title: Australians will pay more if Albanese fast-tracks fossil fuel projects, former oil and gas leaders warn
events.description: Exclusive: Government should back projects that prioritise renewables to protect consumers from ongoing price shocks, they say Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former oil and gas industry leaders, including senior executives from BP and Shell, are warning the Albanese government that Australians risk ongoing price shocks and higher costs if it prioritises fossil fuel development in response to the global energy crisis. Sixteen ex-executives and professionals – who had worked for companies including Woodside, Inpex, Exxon Mobil and Esso – have urged the government to reject calls for fast-tracked gas and coal extraction, arguing it would do nothing to improve the nation’s liquid fuel security. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australians will pay more if Albanese fast-tracks fossil fuel projects, former oil and gas leaders warn

events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day
events.description: The United Nations has condemned two recent drone attacks in Sudan, one of which left seven dead, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Monday during his regular media briefing in New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day

events.title: Trump admin warns Iraq over Iran terror proxies as US reportedly blocks cash payments
events.description: The Trump administration reportedly halted dollar shipments to Baghdad to pressure Iraq into disbanding Iran-backed militias that have attacked U.S. personnel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump admin warns Iraq over Iran terror proxies as US reportedly blocks cash payments

events.title: Iran war: Mojtaba Khamenei says there will be 'change' in Strait of Hormuz 'management'
events.description: Iran's supreme leader issued a statement implying that Tehran wanted to keep its nuclear program and perhaps impose tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. Crude prices remain volatile. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Mojtaba Khamenei says there will be 'change' in Strait of Hormuz 'management'

events.title: Death toll from Colombia bus bombing rises to 20 during wave of violence
events.description: Officials say 20 people died after an explosive device detonated on a bus in southwestern Colombia's Cauca region, injuring 36 others Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Death toll from Colombia bus bombing rises to 20 during wave of violence

events.title: Mali crisis deepens as separatists and jihadists step up attacks
events.description: Mali’s military launched strikes on Kidal after separatist Azawad Liberation Front fighters claimed control, while coordinated attacks with jihadist group JNIM intensified insecurity. As rebels push to expand their reach and demand the withdrawal of Russian forces, jihadists have also announced a blockade of roads into the capital Bamako, raising fears of further escalation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mali crisis deepens as separatists and jihadists step up attacks

events.title: Inquiry into antisemitic attack that left 15 dead in Sydney recommends gun reform
events.description: A government inquiry into a rise in antisemitism across Australia before two gunmen believed to be inspired by the Islamic State group allegedly killed 15 people at a Sydney Jewish festival in December has recommended that authorities prioritize gun re...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Inquiry into antisemitic attack that left 15 dead in Sydney recommends gun reform

events.title: From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks
events.description: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to send shockwaves through global food systems, the UN Food and Agriculture Organization has warned, as a widening crisis across the Middle East continues to strain humanitarian operations and deepen instability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks

events.title: ‘Act of piracy’: World reacts to Israeli interception of Gaza aid flotilla
events.description: World leaders condemn the interception of the boats bound for Gaza as violating international law.

events.title: FBI reveals Brown University mass shooter began plotting massacre as early as 2022, blamed victims
events.description: The FBI concluded its investigation into the Brown University mass shooting and MIT professor murder, revealing the gunman began plotting in 2022.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 FBI reveals Brown University mass shooter began plotting massacre as early as 2022, blamed victims

events.title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism
events.description: Two men were stabbed in north London's Barnet borough, prompting a counterterrorism-led investigation into a potentially antisemitic incident.
events.ticker_label: 💥 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism

events.title: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
events.description: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds

events.title: Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany
events.description: Media tycoon honoured in absentia as critics decry his 20-year sentence under national security law The jailed media entrepreneur Jimmy Lai has been awarded Deutsche Welle’s freedom of speech award for his contribution to Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement. The German public broadcaster said on Thursday that Lai would be presented in absentia with the 12th iteration of the award on 23 June at the DW Global Media Forum in Bonn. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany

events.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
events.description: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
events.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political affairs official Khaled Khiari warned of increasingly dire conditions in Gaza and the West Bank, while former UK Prime Minister and US-led Board of Peace member, Tony Blair, said the council needs to mobilise funds for recovery at this “pivotal juncture”. You can follow all the UN's meetings coverage here but follow live and in-depth updates below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns

events.title: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
events.description: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine

events.title: Over half of South Sudan’s population faces acute hunger crisis
events.description: Conflict and displacement are intensifying South Sudan’s hunger crisis, with 7.8 million people facing high levels of acute food insecurity while 2.2 million children are suffering from acute malnutrition, according to a joint statement on Tuesday from UN agencies.

events.title: Florida lawmakers approve new voting maps to favour Republicans
events.description: The state is the latest to act in a national redistricting war that could shape what political party controls the US House of Representatives.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Florida lawmakers approve new voting maps to favour Republicans

events.title: Why are Western feminists silent on the war on Iranian women?
events.description: Those who backed Iranian women’s protests against compulsory hijab are silent as those same women are killed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why are Western feminists silent on the war on Iranian women?

events.title: World press freedom hits new low as authoritarianism rises
events.description: With three in four countries "problematic" or worse, the 2026 World Press Freedom Index offers a bleak picture for global media. The conditions for press freedom are rated "satisfactory" in only a few dozen countries.

events.title: With mass evacuation warnings, Israel upends lives and reshapes south Lebanon
events.description: The warnings to flee come suddenly: Texts pinging thousands of phones, automated calls from strange numbers, hard-to-read maps shared on social media
events.ticker_label: 🪖 With mass evacuation warnings, Israel upends lives and reshapes south Lebanon

events.title: WHO calls for stepped up action to eliminate viral hepatitis
events.description: Countries are making measurable progress in combatting viral hepatitis, but the disease remains a major global health challenge, the World Health Organization (WHO) said in a new report published on Tuesday.

events.title: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital
events.description: The blockade comes days after Mali's defence minister was assassinated in a series of nationwide attacks.

events.title: Student’s alleged jailing in China over Australian pro-democracy protests sparks calls for inquiry
events.description: Human rights commissioner says alleged jailing highlights the ‘growing risks of transnational repression’ in Australia Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s human rights commissioner has said the Chinese student who was allegedly jailed for six years by Chinese authorities for joining protests in Sydney underscores the “very real and growing risks of transnational repression affecting people in Australia – including international students”. Commissioner Lorraine Finlay told Guardian Australia that while she could not comment on the circumstances of individual cases “no one should fear punishment abroad for exercising their lawful rights to free expression and peaceful protest here”. Continue reading...

events.title: Solar power soars to new heights but fears over 'dangerously high' temperatures
events.description: Almost the entirety of Europe saw above-average temperatures last year, while wildfires burnt a record area and heatwaves struck from north to south, according to an extensive new climate study.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Solar power soars to new heights but fears over 'dangerously high' temperatures

events.title: As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief
events.description: With top diplomats gathered at UN Headquarters to review the 1970 Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), the UN Secretary-General warned that it must evolve to survive the age of AI and other new technologies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief

events.title: Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report
events.description: Human rights groups allege Russia recruits tens of thousands of vulnerable migrants from over 130 countries to fight in its ongoing war against Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report

events.title: 89-year-old man allegedly opens fire with shotgun in Greek capital, injuring 4
events.description: An 89-year-old man allegedly opened fire inside two Athens government buildings, wounding at least four people in a rare shooting incident in Greece.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 89-year-old man allegedly opens fire with shotgun in Greek capital, injuring 4

events.title: Israel kills nine people in southern Lebanon despite ‘ceasefire’
events.description: Israeli attacks and threats on southern Lebanon escalate, as Lebanese government calls out Israel's ceasefire violations
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel kills nine people in southern Lebanon despite ‘ceasefire’

events.title: Will the Iran war reshape the global energy order?
events.description: As oil markets shift, OPEC's grip is under pressure, US exports rise, and China drives the push towards renewables.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Will the Iran war reshape the global energy order?

events.title: Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid
events.description: Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOs The volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them. Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing transportation costs. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid

events.title: Belgium seeks nationalization of nuclear power plants
events.description: Belgium is reversing its decadeslong phasing-out course, seeking more energy independence by reviving its nuclear plants.

events.title: Iran's supreme leader says Tehran will protect its nuclear and missile capabilities
events.description: Iran's supreme leader says the Islamic Republic will protect its nuclear and missile capabilities as a national asset
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's supreme leader says Tehran will protect its nuclear and missile capabilities

events.title: Bank of England expected to hold interest rates as it weighs the impact of Iran war
events.description: The Bank of England is keeping its main interest rate on hold at 3.75% as policymakers assess the economic impact of the Iran war and Tehran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s crude passes
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bank of England expected to hold interest rates as it weighs the impact of Iran war

events.title: World News in Brief: Europe warming twice as fast, acute hunger in Lebanon, peacekeepers deployed in Congo
events.description: New data shows record heatwaves from the Mediterranean to the Arctic last year, with glaciers shrinking and snow cover declining, according to the UN World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Europe warming twice as fast, acute hunger in Lebanon, peacekeepers deployed in Congo

events.title: Court sentences former world leader to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest, other charges
events.description: Ousted South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to seven years in prison after martial law decree that triggered a severe political crisis.

events.title: The sub-Arctic town pitching itself as Canada's gateway to Europe
events.description: Churchill, Manitoba, best known as the world's polar bear capital, has ambitions of making its deep-water port a year-round trade route.

events.title: World’s top media outlets urge Israel to allow independent access to Gaza
events.description: Israel continues to deny foreign reporters access to the Gaza Strip despite a 'ceasefire' being in place.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World’s top media outlets urge Israel to allow independent access to Gaza

events.title: Mexico’s cartel crackdown hits top ranks – but will it fuel Jalisco violence?
events.description: Arrest of potential next leader found hiding in drainage pipe highlights renewed tactics – and fears of cartel infighting US politics live – latest updates The golden coffin of “El Mencho” , the late leader of the Jalisco New Generation cartel (CJNG), had barely been lowered into the ground when the Mexican military dealt a second blow to the very top of the organisation this week. As special forces descended on a ranch in the state of Nayarit, grainy drone footage showed El Mencho’s possible successor, Audias Flores, alias “El Jardinero”, being hauled from a drainage pipe he had tried to hide in, all without a shot being fired. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mexico’s cartel crackdown hits top ranks – but will it fuel Jalisco violence?

events.title: U.K. calls antisemitism an emergency after arson and stabbing attacks on Jewish people
events.description: The British government pledged to increase security for Jewish communities after a string of arson attacks and a double stabbing. But members of the community lashed out at the government.

events.title: Mali holds tribute for assassinated defence minister under tight security
events.description: Mali held a tribute ceremony for its assassinated defence minister in Bamako on Thursday under tight security. Sadio Camara was killed in a rebel bomb attack over the weekend as fighters from a Tuareg separatist group and an al Qaeda-linked jihadist group launched an assault against the military junta.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mali holds tribute for assassinated defence minister under tight security

events.title: Bondi attack inquiry calls for greater security at Jewish events
events.description: An inquiry into the Bondi Beach shooting has called for increased security at Jewish public events and further gun reforms in Australia.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Bondi attack inquiry calls for greater security at Jewish events

events.title: Iran war has fuelled 'boom' in renewable power, says UN climate chief
events.description: The conflict in Iran has "supercharged" the "boom" in renewable power, the United Nations' climate chief has said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war has fuelled 'boom' in renewable power, says UN climate chief

events.title: UK vows to tackle antisemitism 'emergency' as police probe double stabbing attack
events.description: The British government has declared antisemitism in the U.K. an "emergency” and announced plans to spend millions on increasing security around Jewish sites

events.title: Central Asia celebrates 20 years as a nuclear-weapon-free zone
events.description: Semipalatinsk, Kazakhstan was once the Soviet Union’s primary testing ground for nuclear weapons. Today, in an age of rising nuclear threats, the Semipalatinsk Treaty – which saw a group of Central Asian countries renounce nuclear weapons in 2006 – is more relevant than ever.

events.title: UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict
events.description: The UN’s top human rights official warned on Wednesday that Iranians’ rights are being eroded in “harsh and brutal ways,” citing a surge in executions, mass arrests and alleged abuses amid a widening crackdown on dissent during the ongoing conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict

events.title: Iran eyes revenge for Soleimani as WHCA Dinner shooting exposes security ‘vulnerability,’ expert warns
events.description: A former Pentagon intelligence officer claims the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner shooting exposed serious security vulnerabilities around Trump.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran eyes revenge for Soleimani as WHCA Dinner shooting exposes security ‘vulnerability,’ expert warns

events.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
events.description: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
events.description: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents. It said in a statement that its troops in the desert town of Kidal near the Algerian border had fought for more than 24 hours while completely surrounded and vastly outnumbered. It also alleged, without providing evidence, that the militants had been trained by European mercenary instructors, including Ukrainians. The casualty toll was not specified. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns

events.title: ‘Historic breakthrough’: Colombia climate talks end with hopes raised for fossil fuel phaseout
events.description: Nearly 60 countries back voluntary roadmaps to wean world off coal, oil and gas, at conference prompted by frustration with UN climate summits Business live – latest updates Europe live – latest updates Governments have been asked to develop national “roadmaps” setting out how they will end the production and use of fossil fuels, after a landmark climate meeting involving nearly 60 countries. The voluntary plans will form the bedrock of a new initiative to wean the world off coal, oil and gas , the focus of two days of intensive talks in Colombia this week. Continue reading...

events.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
events.description: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon

events.title: Israel stops Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters
events.description: More than 20 ships were intercepted in a mission aimed at challenging the blockade of the Gaza Strip. Israel dismissed those on board as "attention-seeking agitators."

events.title: 'Growing sense in international community' that Trump doesn't have an Iran exit strategy
events.description: Amid fresh transatlantic tensions surrounding Iran, NATO, and US troops in Germany, Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Dr Christopher Featherstone, Associate Lecturer in Politics and International Relations at the University of York. Dr Featherstone sees a strained alliance caught between performance and paralysis. He describes Trump as not simply erratic, but as deliberately “provocative” and “unpredictable,” arguing that “there is a fear on the European side when it comes to this Trump administration in just how unpredictable he is: You don't want to signal a willingness to get involved when you don't know where that involvement is going to lead."

events.title: Hormuz disruption deepens energy shock through vulnerable regions
events.description: With no end in sight to the shipping stand-off in the Strait of Hormuz and as Brent crude oil prices hovered at around $118 in trading on Thursday, the prospect of running out of fuel, gas and more has focused attention in the world’s capitals on finding solutions, quickly.

events.title: North Korea faces food shortages as drought threatens crops
events.description: The country's state-run news has said much of the country has been hit by an unusually severe drought. The largely isolated country, with a population of over 26 million, was already facing food shortages.

events.title: Energy fears drive Gulf mediation in US-Iran conflict
events.description: Gulf countries are quietly pressuring the United States and Iran to reach a deal as the conflict disrupts vital oil exports and takes a growing economic toll. Working behind the scenes, including through Pakistan, they are trying to revive negotiations despite tensions with Tehran as FRANCE 24’s Hoda Abdel Hamid reports from Doha.

events.title: Myanmar army shifts to offense as resistance weakens in bloody civil war
events.description: A little more than a year ago, the government’s military was on the back foot in Myanmar’s bloody civil war, pushed out of great swaths of the country’s north by an alliance of seasoned militias, and forced into defensive action around the rest of the ...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Myanmar army shifts to offense as resistance weakens in bloody civil war

events.title: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 women teachers and health workers
events.description: Restrictions on girls’ education and women’s employment in Afghanistan could leave the country with a deficit of over 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 women teachers and health workers

events.title: Stolen chemical-dispersing drones could have facilitated 'action against the homeland,' fmr DHS official warns
events.description: Federal authorities recovered 15 stolen industrial drones capable of dispersing liquid chemicals, but a former DHS official says key questions remain.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Stolen chemical-dispersing drones could have facilitated 'action against the homeland,' fmr DHS official warns

events.title: Iran turmoil erupts: Ultra-hardliner who mocked Trump poised to take over nuclear talks
events.description: Reports say veteran hardliner Saeed Jalili may replace Ghalibaf on Iran's negotiating team, deepening fractures within the regime's political leadership.

events.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
events.description: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

events.title: Anti-immigrant attacks and protests in South Africa
events.description: Anti-immigrant attacks and protests in South Africa

events.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
events.description: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act

events.title: Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
events.description: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine

events.title: DR Congo creates paramilitary mining guard
events.description: Democratic Republic of the Congo has announced the creation of a paramilitary force to secure its mining sector, backed by funding from the United States and United Arab Emirates. The unit, expected to grow to over 20,000 personnel by 2028, aims to protect operations, strengthen state control and reassure investors as global competition for critical minerals intensifies. FRANCE 24's Emmet Livingstone has the details from Kinshasa.

events.title: RAF troops shot down more than 100 Iranian drones and missiles fired at Middle East base
events.description: A coalition base in Iraq that British forces are helping to defend was targeted by around 28 Iranian-linked drones and missiles a day as the US and Israel attacked Iran, it has emerged.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 RAF troops shot down more than 100 Iranian drones and missiles fired at Middle East base

events.title: Hezbollah adopts a new weapon: Fiber-optic drones, used widely in the war in Ukraine
events.description: The Israeli military is dealing with a new threat from Hezbollah

events.title: US military equipment worth billions of dollars destroyed in Iran war

events.title: Viral pro-Iran AI memes blur lines between propaganda and entertainment
events.description: Iranian-American creator Mahdi Hemmat is gaining millions of views with AI-generated, Lego-style rap videos that blend satire, pop culture and political messaging about the conflict involving Iran, the United States and Israel. While presented as entertainment, the content promotes pro-Iran narratives and criticism of Donald Trump, illustrating how digital creators are shaping opinion and reaching younger Western audiences online.

events.title: ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards
events.description: Delegates at event in Cape Verde highlight opportunities from tech while stressing AI is no replacement for talent Last July, the Nigerian singer-songwriter Fave found herself caught up in a viral moment: an unauthorised version of a track by her featuring an AI choir had been released, quickly becoming an internet sensation. To get ahead of the situation, she recorded her own remix that integrated the AI-assisted song and added it to her discography. “In my view, [that] was smart and very business aware,” Oyinkansola Fawehinmi, a Lagos-based entertainment lawyer, observed a few months later . “She essentially reclaimed the ‘AI version’ and released it as her own official expression.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards

events.title: Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court
events.description: Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslims said poor mental health made him admit to crimes The Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques in Christchurch in 2019 has been prevented from appealing against his guilty pleas, after one of New Zealand’s highest courts said his bid was “utterly devoid of merit”. Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to appeal against his guilty pleas, claiming harsh prison conditions had affected his mental health and compelled him to admit to the crimes. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court

events.title: Canada to create powerful financial crimes agency as US weakens its approach
events.description: Cryptocurrency ATMs also face ban, after public inquiry found Canada lacked anti-money-laundering strategy Canada is to establish a new and powerful law enforcement agency to investigate financial crime, in stark contrast to the US, where weakened federal investigators have struggled to pursue fraudsters and the White House has pardoned convicted money launderers . A bill to create the Financial Crimes Agency (FCA) completed its first reading in parliament this week. The legislation was introduced by the governing Liberals and with their parliamentary majority, the party is likely to move it through both levels of government quickly. Continue reading...

events.title: Limiting capital gains tax changes to new investments would ‘severely delay’ budget reforms, Deloitte says
events.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers had indicated ‘transitional’ proposed changes as Labor attempts to repair a ‘structurally flawed’ budget Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Only applying changes to the CGT discount and negative gearing rules to new investments would “severely delay” desperately needed reforms required to repair a “structurally flawed” budget and boost the economy, Deloitte says. The consulting firm estimated that a policy which cut the 50% capital gains tax discount to 33% and abolished negative gearing would only generate $500m over the first four years of operation if existing investments were not included – an approach known as “grandfathering”. Continue reading...

Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February. Continue reading...

events.title: More private health records of UK Biobank volunteers appear on Chinese website

Some officials were members of Mexico’s progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum as she seeks to offset mounting pressures from the Trump administration. events.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
events.description: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin. During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. events.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
events.description: Leader mentions for first time lengths to which troops go to avoid falling into enemy hands while fighting for Russia Kim Jong-un has praised North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up with grenades in order to avoid capture while fighting Ukrainian forces in Russia’s western Kursk region, confirming the existence of the extreme battlefield policy. Mounting evidence, including from intelligence reports and testimonies of defectors, has indicated North Korean soldiers are explicitly told to resort to self-detonation or other forms of suicide to avoid falling into enemy hands. events.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
events.description: Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.

events.title: The Austrian nuns who fled their care home are now in Rome and visited the Vatican
events.description: The three octogenarian nuns, who made headlines last year after they broke back into their convent, joined others at St. Peter's Square for a general audience with Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday morning.

events.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
events.description: An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

events.title: UAE oil exit from OPEC signals focus on domestic priorities
events.description: Abu Dhabi's exit from OPEC signals a geopolitical shift and a pointed message to Saudi Arabia. What are the ripple effects on ties with Riyadh, the US, and Israel in the short and long term?

events.title: Iran's Vafaei playing for more than snooker glory
events.description: After years lost to visa battles, Hossein Vafaei is finally delivering on his early promise as a snooker prodigy. He's hoping to inspire people back home amid the turmoil in Iran.

events.title: Study busts myth: German 'spring fatigue' doesn't exist
events.description: Do we really feel more tired in spring? Researchers in Switzerland say, "there's no such thing as spring fatigue." But it is a deep cultural phenomenon in German-speaking countries.

events.title: First dots on the roadmap to exiting fossil fuels
events.description: The message from inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels was clear. It's not if, but when and how.

events.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
events.description: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

events.title: Women journalists in Ghana encounter – and overcome – gender barriers
events.description: In Ghana, DW Akademie works with women journalists as they try to advance in their careers despite gender barriers.

events.title: Ecuador starts fracking in Amazon rainforest
events.description: Ecuador is pushing to increase its production of crude oil but faces a backlash from environmentalists and Indigenous groups.

events.title: Booker Prize nominee Chigozie Obioma on being an African writer today
events.description: In this edition we head to Morocco for the Marrakech African Book Festival (FLAM), a key gathering for writers, thinkers and novelists from across Africa and its diaspora. Our team sits down with Nigerian writer Chigozie Obioma, whose acclaimed works "The Fishermen" and "An Orchestra of Minorities" were both shortlisted for the Booker Prize.

events.title: Russia’s role in Mali questioned as rebels push for exit
events.description: In Mali, rebels have called for Russian forces to withdraw, with the pullback from Kidal highlighting growing pressure despite Moscow’s claims of success and its habit of blaming external actors. For Jumo Ayandela, specialist in African politics and security at the New York University, this reflects a wider narrative battle increasingly questioning Russia’s role across the Sahel, while the interview also covers Mali’s leadership, jihadist forces and technological capabilities.

events.title: Are fans being priced out of live gigs?
events.description: A spate of high-profile stadium tours by stars like Céline Dion, Taylor Swift and Oasis have put a spotlight on the practice of dynamic ticket pricing, with many fans saying they're unable to secure or afford tickets. We take a closer look in this edition of People & Profit.

events.title: Study finds Indonesian hotspots are 'like home' for endangered whale sharks
events.description: Two bays in Indonesia are hotspots for endangered whale sharks, who frequent their waters year-round and not seasonally as previously thought, a new study has revealed. The research tracked 70 whale sharks over a decade, shedding new light on their migration habits.

events.title: Drug kingpin jailed after extradition from Dubai
events.description: A gangster who played a "key" role in a multi-million-pound drugs operation has been jailed for six years after being extradited to Scotland from the United Arab Emirates.

events.title: Myanmar reduces ousted leader Suu Kyi's prison term in new amnesty
events.description: Myanmar’s military-backed government shortened the prison sentence of ousted leader Aung San Suu Kyi

events.title: Activists say Israeli forces intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete, detaining crews
events.description: Activists attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of Gaza say Israeli forces have intercepted their flotilla near the southern Greek island of Crete

events.title: French teen charged in Singapore over a vending machine straw-licking video
events.description: A French teen faces charges in Singapore after posting a video of himself licking a straw from an orange juice vending machine and putting it back

events.title: Brazil's Senate blocks Lula's Supreme Court nominee, first rejection in 132 years
events.description: Brazil’s Senate dealt a political blow to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Wednesday by rejecting his appointee to the Supreme Court, the first in more than 130 years

events.title: Canada will be the headquarters for a future NATO-linked financial institution, official says
events.description: A senior government official says that Canada has been selected as the headquarters for a new, financial institution led by NATO and designed to reduce borrowing costs for members of the alliance

events.title: Brazil's Senate rejects president's Supreme Court nominee for first time in more than 100 years
events.description: Brazil's Senate rejects president's Supreme Court nominee for first time in more than 100 years

events.title: Bodycam shows dad bolt from cops with young child in his arms before ditching her alone in the woods
events.description: Body camera footage shows a Florida man allegedly fleeing Daytona Beach police with his child before leaving the girl alone in the woods to evade arrest.

events.title: Surveillance photos show Secret Service agents firing at Trump's alleged would-be assassin inside DC hotel
events.description: Surveillance video shows Secret Service agents firing at alleged would-be assassin Cole Allen outside the Washington Hilton ballroom hosting Trump.

events.title: Cold case suspect nabbed overseas after DNA revived decades-old child abduction mystery
events.description: A man living in the Philippines faces charges after authorities say DNA solved a nearly 40-year-old cold case involving a child kidnapped in Florida.

events.title: Maritime spies catch $800M oil scheme as Trump's naval blockade squeezes Tehran and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Georgia man says he suffered severe medical neglect leading to amputations at scrutinized Atlanta jail
events.description: A Georgia man said he lost his fingers and lower legs to amputation after allegedly being denied critical medical care at the Fulton County Jail.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Georgia man says he suffered severe medical neglect leading to amputations at scrutinized Atlanta jail

events.title: Illegal immigrant admitted posing as Border Patrol agent in calculated scheme to disrupt deportations: DOJ
events.description: A man accused of impersonating a Border Patrol agent to interfere with ICE operations pleaded guilty and faces up to 15 years on gun charges.

events.title: Suspect arrested after mom, pregnant teen, 12-year-old found bound and murdered
events.description: Authorities arrested William Graham Oliver in the murders of three family members found bound in an Alabama home, including two children.

events.title: Louisiana sheriff charged after toilet jailbreak let 10 inmates escape in lockup fiasco
events.description: Louisiana Sheriff Susan Hutson was indicted on 30 counts, including obstruction of justice and malfeasance, stemming from a major New Orleans jailbreak.

events.title: Kohberger defense team says it is 'appalled' by former expert's public comments about Idaho murders case
events.description: Bryan Kohberger's defense team says former expert Brent Turvey broke his confidentiality agreement by speaking publicly about the Idaho murders case. He denies wrongdoing.

events.title: Iran’s $800M oil smuggling scheme uses tankers posing as Iraqi ships to dodge blockade
events.description: Sanctioned tankers are allegedly disguising Iranian oil shipments as Iraqi cargo while Trump doubles down on the naval blockade to squeeze Tehran.

events.title: Record antisemitic incidents in Canada fuel criticism of Carney government response
events.description: Canada faces record antisemitic incidents in 2025 as critics say Prime Minister Mark Carney's government is failing to adequately address anti-Jewish hate.

events.title: Could Narges Mohammadi unite Iran’s opposition? Husband says imprisoned Nobel laureate still fighting
events.description: Narges Mohammadi's husband says the jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate was severely beaten during arrest but remains politically unbroken in prison.
